name: Dr Cheng Ye
focus: Planetary spectroscopy • Surface composition • Radiative transfer modeling
bio: Cheng Ye is a planetary scientist at Northern Arizona University whose research focuses on understanding planetary surface composition and geological processes through advanced remote sensing techniques. His work specializes in visible, near-infrared, and thermal infrared spectroscopy, which he uses to analyze the physical and chemical properties of planetary materials. Dr. Ye integrates laboratory experiments, theoretical modeling, and spacecraft-based observations to interpret remote sensing data and improve our understanding of planetary environments such as Mars. His research also includes radiative transfer and light scattering, contributing to more accurate analysis of spectral data collected from orbiting instruments. Through his interdisciplinary approach, he plays a key role in advancing techniques used to study planetary surfaces and their evolution across the solar system.
